# Spec context: §10.1 — Outdated information field syntax

## Source

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#outdated_information_field_syntax>
- Cached at: `crates/kaijutsu-abc/docs/abc-spec-cache.md` (line 2231)

- Subsection: 10.1 Outdated information field syntax

## Preceding prose

> The `A:` [field](#aarea "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") was originally used to contain area information. In version 2.0 this was changed to contain the name of the lyrics author. In version 2.1, to maintain backwards compatibility, this has been changed back to area, but for clarity, the `A:` field is [depre
> *Comment:* Of the 160,000 tunes currently available in the [abcnotation.com tune search](https://abcnotation.com/search "https://abcnotation.com/search") 16,300 contain an `A:` field with 680 distinct values. Of these, only around 10 contain author information rather than area (in some cases it is d
> An `E:` field was once used by `abc2mtex` to explicitly control note spacing; this is no longer necessary with current formatting algorithms and the `E:` field is now [deprecated](#outdated_syntax "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵").
> The original usage of the `H:` [history](#hhistory "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") field, where the contents of the history field is considered to continue over several lines until the next field occurs, is now [deprecated](#outdated_syntax "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵").
> The `Q:` [tempo](#qtempo "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") field is still very much in use, but earlier versions of the standard permitted two syntax variants, now [deprecated](#outdated_syntax "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"), which specified how many unit note lengths to play per minute.
> *Examples:* Both examples mean "play 120 unit note-lengths per minute".
